title: North Fork
subtitle: Eden, Utah
summary:
## The Hike In

Four miles in on the North Fork River Trail out of Eden. Spring runoff was running strong — multiple water crossings on the way in. The canyon views through the trees made every crossing worth it.

Keishin slipped on the trail three times. Legendary.

## Camp

Hilltop site with flat, level ground and genuine 360° views. Rocky terrain, but sleeping pads handled it — thicker pads would be a step up next time. Pine View Reservoir visible to the south, snow-capped southwest peaks on the horizon, and the canyon stretching back toward the trailhead.

Clear skies, no clouds. Beautiful spring weather.

## Wildlife

Spotted a bird on the trail — roughly a foot tall, likely a chukar partridge or pheasant. Didn't stick around long.

## Gear Notes

The two camp chairs were a great call — highly recommended going forward.

The bear can had an incident: chicken packaging ruptured, spilling liquid on gear and all over Keishin's hands. Classic. LifeStraw handled water filtration but the water source was farther than expected — we had just enough. Perrin's sleeping pad is broken and needs to be replaced before the next trip.

## Lessons Learned

- **Pre-cook chicken at home** — raw chicken is hard to handle, dirties hands, and the smell defeats the purpose of the bear can
- **Double-bag any raw or liquid food** in the bear can
- Don't forget the spice kit — there's a camping container for it, just remember to pack it
- Bring collapsible camping bowls / plates
- Bring hot chocolate
- Bring a thermometer
- Add a repair kit to the checklist
- Bring a ground tarp / case sheet
